results.title: NASA Standard 8739.4: Wiring Guidelines | PDF
results.description: The document provides standards for crimping, interconnecting cables, harnesses, and wiring for NASA. It establishes requirements for reliable crimped connections and documentation of processes.
results.content:
The document provides standards for crimping, interconnecting cables, harnesses, and wiring for NASA. It establishes requirements for reliable crimped connections and documentation of processes.
The document provides standards for crimping, interconnecting cables, harnesses, and wiring for NASA. It establishes requirements for reliable crimped connections and documentation of processes. It covers training and certification programs for personnel, facilities, equipment, materials, design practices, temporary identification, protection, and forming wires into cables and harnesses using various methods like lacing, braiding, spiral wrapping, and strapping.

results.title: Nasa std-8739-4 | PDF
results.description: This document provides standards for crimping, interconnecting cables, harnesses, and wiring for NASA. It establishes requirements for personnel training and certification programs.
results.content:
This document provides standards for crimping, interconnecting cables, harnesses, and wiring for NASA. It establishes requirements for personnel training and certification programs.
This document provides standards for crimping, interconnecting cables, harnesses, and wiring for NASA. It establishes requirements for personnel training and certification programs. The document defines terms and applicable specifications. It covers general principles for reliable cabling and wiring, documentation, and rework procedures.
This Standard cancels NASA Assurance Standard (NAS) 5300.4(3G-1), Workmanship Standard for Interconnecting Cables, Harnesses, and Wiring, and NASA Handbook (NHB) 5300.4(3H), Requirements for Crimping and Wire Wrap. This Standard shall not be rewritten or reissued in any other form not approved by NASA. Other processes not covered by this standard may be required. The design, materials, and processes shall be defined in engineering documentation. ... NASA-STD-8739.4 with Change6 Page 7 of 114 Comments and suggestions for improving this Standard may be submitted using the form “NASA Technical Standard Improvement Proposal.” A copy of this form is included at the end of this standard.
Crimping of solder tinned wire is prohibited. 4.3.5 Design Considerations. The basic design considerations to assure reliable interconnecting cable and wire assemblies are as follows: a. query.original: NASA-STD-8739.4 crimping interconnecting cables harnesses wiring PDF

results.question: How does NASA-STD-8739.4 ensure the reliability of interconnecting cable and wire assemblies?
results.answer: The Standard ensures reliability by mandating that materials, parts, and hardware are chosen for proper fit and support, tools should not damage the wires during assembly, wires must not be taut, wiring must be inspected post-assembly for conformance, and protection against shock, vibration, and abrasion must be incorporated .
results.title: NASA Standard 8739.4: Wiring Guidelines | PDF

results.question: What are the key components of a training program for crimping, cabling, and harnessing personnel outlined in the NASA-STD-8739.4?
results.answer: The training program must identify qualification and certification criteria for Level B instructors, operators, and inspectors, document methods and procedures to meet this Standard, use visual standards like samples and illustrations, and provide access to these standards. Records of training must be maintained for 5 years .
results.title: NASA Standard 8739.4: Wiring Guidelines | PDF

results.question: What documentation is required from suppliers to meet NASA-STD-8739.4 standards for cable and harness fabrication?
results.answer: Suppliers must provide a documented training program, including instructor qualifications, lesson plans, training hours, and certification procedures. This documentation must be retained for five years and made available for review on request .
results.title: NASA Standard 8739.4: Wiring Guidelines | PDF

results.question: How does NASA-STD-8739.4 address the importance of using specific types of sleeving in wiring installations?
results.answer: The Standard mandates the use of specific sleeving types, like heat-shrinkable, to ensure tight coverage over wires, prevent flexing-induced failures, and protect against environmental factors. Sleeving must be applied to meet precise dimensional requirements .
results.title: NASA Standard 8739.4: Wiring Guidelines | PDF

results.question: How is quality assurance in cable and harness fabrication maintained according to NASA-STD-8739.4?
results.answer: Quality assurance is maintained by ensuring appropriate tool use to avoid damage, thorough documentation of processes, mandatory training and certification of personnel, and rigorous inspection of final assemblies as per engineering documentation standards .
results.title: NASA Standard 8739.4: Wiring Guidelines | PDF

results.question: In what ways must plastic straps and metal braid shielding be installed to comply with NASA standards?
results.answer: Plastic straps must be locked, spaced correctly, not overly tight, and installed using tension-controlled tooling. Metal braid shielding should be pre-cleaned, tightened to the wire bundle, and may require a separator under the shielding to protect wire insulation .
results.title: NASA Standard 8739.4: Wiring Guidelines | PDF

results.question: Why is it crucial for connectors to be assembled as per engineering documentation in compliance with NASA-STD-8739.4?
results.answer: Connector assembly as per documentation prevents issues like improper seating of contacts, which causes open circuits and unreliable connections. Proper assembly ensures environmental protection and stress relief in high-moisture interfaces .
results.title: NASA Standard 8739.4: Wiring Guidelines | PDF

results.question: What are the criteria for rejection of a cable assembly as specified by NASA-STD-8739.4?
results.answer: Rejection criteria include damaged connectors, non-conforming pin/sockets retention force, uneven tension distribution, improper wire twist length, incorrect or damaging ties, improper bend radii, improper use or absence of protective coverings, and incorrect soldering techniques .
results.title: NASA Standard 8739.4: Wiring Guidelines | PDF

results.question: Describe the procedural steps and requirements for recertifying NASA Level B instructors according to NASA-STD-8739.4.
results.answer: Recertification of Level B instructors involves retraining by a Level A instructor, when proficiency standards are unmet, new techniques are introduced, there is a work gap longer than six months, or two years have passed since last certification .
results.title: NASA Standard 8739.4: Wiring Guidelines | PDF

results.question: What vision requirements must personnel meet according to NASA-STD-8739.4, and why are these important?
results.answer: Personnel must meet specific vision requirements: a Snellen Chart 20/50 for far vision, Jaeger 1 at 14 inches for near vision, and the ability to distinguish key colors using color vision tests. These requirements are vital to ensure accurate fabrication and inspection of wires and cables .
results.title: NASA Standard 8739.4: Wiring Guidelines | PDF
